behavior_in_task_description.reason: The test test_playwright_tutorial_summon asserts 'extensionName="Summon"' must appear in the file, but instruction.md only says 'Update any code blocks or component references that use the old extension name to use the new one' without specifying the exact string 'extensionName="Summon"'. Also, test_summon_mcp_example_content requires exact section headers '## Configuration' and '## Example Usage', and requires 'subagent' in lowercase — instruction says 'Include a section on how to configure/ena

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what needs to change (rename Skills to Summon, create new doc, add deprecation notice, update references) but does not specify exact line numbers, sed commands, or literal replacement strings. The agent must reason about what content to write.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact strings like '## Configuration', '## Example Usage', 'extensionName="Summon"', 'subagent' (lowercase). Alternative correct implementations using '## Setup' or '## How to Configure' or '## Examples' or 'sub-agent' would fail. The test_playwright_tutorial_summon check for 'extensionName="Summon"' is particularly restrictive to the gold solution's specific JSX component attribute format.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_summon_mcp_file_created through test_summon_retro_image) are almost entirely string-in-file checks: 'assert X in content'. The pass-to-pass tests (test_documentation_npm_test, test_documentation_build, test_documentation_verify_build) do execute the build system, but the core behavioral tests are purely text matching with no execution or rendering.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/empty implementation would not pass: summon-mcp.md must be created with >500 chars and specific content, existing files must be modified to contain 'Summon' and 'summon-mcp', and a PNG >1000 bytes must exist. These require substantive work.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/goose as part of the container setup (solve.sh is available). An agent could potentially read the solution directory or grep test files in tests/test_outputs.py to discover exact expected strings like 'extensionName="Summon"', '## Configuration', '## Example Usage', etc. The tests themselves contain all the fixture values needed to reverse-engineer the solution.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All seven tasks from the instruction are tested: summon-mcp creation (tests 1-2), skills-mcp deprecation (test 3), using-extensions update (test 4), using-skills guide (test 5), playwright tutorial (test 6), remotion tutorial (test 7), and screenshot image (test 8). Build tests cover that changes don't break the documentation site.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass repo_tests, pass-to-pass static), descriptive function names (test_summon_mcp_file_created, test_skills_mcp_deprecated, etc.), and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces markdown documentation files, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, extension names, and commands in instruction.md are consistent and match those used in the test suite. No typos detected.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what changes to make at a task level (create file, add deprecation, update references) without specifying exact lines, sed commands, or internal implementation details. File paths are named since this is a documentation editing task where the files are the domain.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a documentation content editing task, not a config-editing task.
